question: Describe the key difference between the database approach and the traditional file processing system.
expectedPoints: Database approach provides a centralized and integrated view of data, reducing data redundancy
File processing systems have decentralized data, leading to isolated files and data duplication
The database approach allows data to be shared by multiple applications, unlike file systems

question: Explain how the database approach supports data abstraction and data independence.
expectedPoints: Data abstraction hides the complexity of the physical storage details from users through levels (physical, logical, view)
Data independence allows changes at one level without affecting the others
Logical data independence protects applications from changes in the logical structure of the data
Physical data independence protects applications from changes in the physical storage structure

question: Discuss the significance of the Self-Describing Nature of a Database System and how it facilitates multiple-user interfaces.
expectedPoints: The system catalog (data dictionary) stores meta-data about the database structure, constraints, and applications
This self-describing nature allows the DBMS to work with different databases without changing its structure
It enables support for multiple user views, where each user can have a customized interface to the same data
Facilitates data sharing and concurrent access by different users and application programs
Provides the foundation for program-data independence, allowing changes to data without affecting application programs
